detections.summary: The agreement caps vendor liability at 12 months of fees, with carve-outs for serious breaches.
detections.extracted_clause: Vendor's aggregate liability arising out of this agreement shall not exceed the fees paid by Customer during the twelve (12) months preceding the claim.

detections.summary: Customer receives assignment of custom-developed IP after payment, while Vendor retains pre-existing tools.
detections.extracted_clause: All intellectual property developed by Vendor specifically for Customer under a statement of work shall be assigned to Customer upon full payment.

detections.summary: Either party can terminate for convenience with 30 days notice, creating continuity risk.
detections.extracted_clause: Either party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice.

detections.summary: Customer has a first-year minimum spend commitment and must pay any shortfall.
detections.extracted_clause: Customer shall purchase a minimum of $250,000 in services during the first contract year.

unknown_risk_candidates.reason: The clause describes handling of customer personal data and breach notification obligations, which is not one of the current 8 mapped risk categories.
unknown_risk_candidates.evidence: Vendor shall notify Customer within seventy-two (72) hours of becoming aware of any security incident involving Customer personal data.
